codeSystems.name: ObservationInterpretationCodes
codeSystems.title: Observation Interpretation Codes
codeSystems.description:
A categorical assessment, providing a rough qualitative interpretation of the observation value,    such as “normal”/ “abnormal”,”low” / “high”, “better” / “worse”, “susceptible” / “resistant”, “expected”/ “not expected”.    The value set is intended to be for ANY use where coded representation of an interpretation is needed.   
   
   Notes:
   
   This is being communicated in v2.x in OBX-8 (Observation Interpretation), in v3 in ObservationInterpretation (CWE) in R1 (Representative Realm) and in FHIR in    Observation.interpretation. Historically these values come from the laboratory domain, and these codes are extensively    used. The value set binding is extensible, so codes outside the value set that are needed for interpretation concepts    (i.e. particular meanings) that are not included in the value set can be used, and these new codes may also be added to    the value set and published in a future version.

codeSystems.name: VitalSigns
codeSystems.title: Vital Signs
codeSystems.description: This value set indicates the allowed vital sign result types.   The LOINC code for Vitals Signs panel (85353-1) is a grouping structure for a set of vital signs and includes related links (with type=has-member) to the Observations in this set (e.g. respiratory rate, heart rate, BP).  The Blood pressure panel (85354-9) is used to group the component observations Systolic blood pressure (8480-6) and Diastolic blood pressure (8462-4).

codeSystems.name: ProcedureNotPerformedReason(SNOMED-CT)
codeSystems.title: Procedure Not Performed Reason (SNOMED-CT)
codeSystems.description: Situation codes describing the reason that a procedure, which might otherwise be expected, was not performed, or a procedure that was started and was not completed. Consists of SNOMED CT codes, children of procedure contraindicated (183932001), procedure discontinued (416406003), procedure not done (416237000), procedure not indicated (428119001), procedure not offered (416064006), procedure not wanted (416432009), procedure refused (183944003), and procedure stopped (394908001).

codeSystems.name: LanguageCodesWithLanguageAndOptionallyARegionModifier
codeSystems.title: Language codes with language and optionally a region modifier
codeSystems.description: This value set includes codes from [BCP-47](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). This value set matches the ONC 2015 Edition LanguageCommunication data element value set within C-CDA to use a 2 character language code if one exists,   and a 3 character code if a 2 character code does not exist. It points back to [RFC 5646](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5646), however only the language codes are required, all other elements are optional.

codeSystems.name: USCoreConditionCategoryCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Condition Category Codes
codeSystems.description: The US Core Condition Category Codes support the separate concepts of problems and health concerns in Condition.category in order for API consumers to be able to separate health concerns and problems. However this is not mandatory for 2015 certification

codeSystems.name: USCoreConditionCode
codeSystems.title: US Core Condition Code
codeSystems.description: This describes the problem. Diagnosis/Problem List is broadly defined as a series of brief statements that catalog a patient's medical, nursing, dental, social, preventative and psychiatric events and issues that are relevant to that patient's healthcare (e.g., signs, symptoms, and defined conditions).   ICD-10 is appropriate for Diagnosis information, and ICD-9 for historical information.

codeSystems.name: USCoreDiagnosticReportCategory
codeSystems.title: US Core DiagnosticReport Category
codeSystems.description: The US Core Diagnostic Report Category Value Set is a 'starter set' of categories supported for fetching and Diagnostic Reports and notes.
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-ach-monthly-diagnost: ACH Monthly Event Diagnostic Report - Report and Note

codeSystems.name: USCoreDiagnosticReportLabCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Diagnostic Report Laboratory Codes
codeSystems.description: The Document Type value set includes all LOINC  values whose CLASSTYPE is LABORATORY in the LOINC database
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-ach-monthly-diagnost: ACH Monthly Event Diagnostic Report (Lab)

codeSystems.name: USCoreDiagnosticreportReportAndNoteCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Diagnosticreport Report And Note Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set currently contains all of LOINC. The codes selected should represent discrete and narrative diagnostic observations and reports
codeSystems.StructureDefinition-ach-monthly-diagnost: ACH Monthly Event Diagnostic Report - Report and Note

codeSystems.name: USCoreEncounterType
codeSystems.title: US Core Encounter Type
codeSystems.description: The type of encounter: a specific code indicating type of service provided. This value set includes codes from SNOMED CT decending from the concept 308335008 (Patient encounter procedure (procedure)) and from the Current Procedure and Terminology(CPT) designated for Evaluation and Management (99200 – 99607) (subscription to AMA Required)

codeSystems.name: USCoreMedicationCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Medication Codes (RxNorm)
codeSystems.description: All prescribable medication formulations represented using  either a 'generic' or 'brand-specific' concept. This includes RxNorm codes whose Term Type is SCD (semantic clinical drug), SBD (semantic brand drug), GPCK (generic pack), BPCK (brand pack), SCDG (semantic clinical drug group), SBDG (semantic brand drug group), SCDF (semantic clinical drug form), or SBDF (semantic brand drug form)

codeSystems.name: USCoreProcedureCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Procedure Codes
codeSystems.description: Concepts from CPT, SNOMED CT, HCPCS Level II Alphanumeric Codes, ICD-10-PCS and CDT code systems that can be used to indicate the type of procedure performed.

codeSystems.name: UspsTwoLetterAlphabeticCodes
codeSystems.title: USPS Two Letter Alphabetic Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set defines two letter USPS alphabetic codes.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSReAdmissionIndicator
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-re-admissionIndicator
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes which are used to specify that a patient is being re-admitted to a healthcare facility from which they were discharged, and indicates the circumstances around such re-admission.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSBedStatus
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-bedStatus
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that specify the state of a bed in an inpatient setting, and is used to determine if a patient may be assigned to it or not.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSAdditivePreservative
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-additivePreservative
codeSystems.description: Concepts specifying any additive introduced to the specimen before or at the time of collection.  These additives may be introduced in order to preserve, maintain or enhance the particular nature or component of the specimen.  Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSSpecimenCondition
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-specimenCondition
codeSystems.description: Concepts of modes or states of being that describe the nature of a specimen.  Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSRelevantClincialInformation
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-relevantClincialInformation
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that specify additional clinical information about the patient or specimen to report the supporting and/or suspected diagnosis and clinical findings on requests for interpreted diagnostic studies.

codeSystems.name: ActEncounterCode
codeSystems.title: ActEncounterCode
codeSystems.description: Domain provides codes that qualify the ActEncounterClass (ENC)

codeSystems.name: ActPriority
codeSystems.title: ActPriority
codeSystems.description:
A code or set of codes (e.g., for routine, emergency,) specifying the urgency under which the Act happened, can happen, is happening, is intended to happen, or is requested/demanded to happen.

*Discussion:* This attribute is used in orders to indicate the ordered priority, and in event documentation it indicates the actual priority used to perform the act. In definition mood it indicates the available priorities.

codeSystems.name: ActSubstanceAdminSubstitutionCode
codeSystems.title: ActSubstanceAdminSubstitutionCode
codeSystems.description: ***No description***

codeSystems.name: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType
codeSystems.title: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType
codeSystems.description: A role of a place that further classifies the setting (e.g., accident site, road side, work site, community location) in which services are delivered.

codeSystems.name: SubstanceAdminSubstitutionReason
codeSystems.title: SubstanceAdminSubstitutionReason
codeSystems.description: ***No description***

codeSystems.name: AcuteCareHospitalDailyEventDiagnosticReportStatus
codeSystems.title: ACH Daily Diagnostic Report Status ValueSet
codeSystems.description: A set of codes indicating the status of the diagnostic report.

codeSystems.name: AcuteCareHospitalDailyEventEncounterClass
codeSystems.title: ACH Daily Encounter Class ValueSet
codeSystems.description: A set of codes indicating the encounter class that represent inpatient, observation and short stay concepts.

codeSystems.name: AcuteCareHospitalDailyEncounterStatus
codeSystems.title: ACH Daily Encounter Status ValueSet
codeSystems.description: A set of codes indicating the status of the encounter.

codeSystems.name: AcuteCareHospitalMonthlyEncounterClass
codeSystems.title: ACH Monthly Encounter Class ValueSet
codeSystems.description: A set of codes indicating the encounter class that represent inpatient, emergency, observation and short stay concepts.

codeSystems.name: AcuteCareHospitalMonthlyEncounterStatus
codeSystems.title: ACH Monthly Encounter Status ValueSet
codeSystems.description: A set of codes indicating the status of the encounter.
